issues.description: Using XMLParser to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace XMLParser with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called

issues.code:
3 import logging
4 from xml.etree.ElementTree import XMLParser, fromstring
5
issues.description: Using XMLParser to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace XMLParser with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
issues.judge_reason: The import of XMLParser from xml.etree.ElementTree is flagged because the standard library XMLParser does not have secure defaults. Although the code defines a subclass to disable external entities, the subclass is not actually used in the fromstring call (the parser argument is ignored by fromstring). Thus the import itself remains a risk indicator.

issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: The code calls fromstring(metadata_xml, parser=NoExternalEntityResolver()) but the NoExternalEntityResolver class is defined but the parser argument is not actually passed correctly because fromstring does not accept a parser argument in the standard xml.etree.ElementTree API. The custom class is instantiated but not used effectively, leaving the parsing potentially vulnerable to XML external entity attacks.
